title: uprmgets
html:
uprmgets

Return Uniface-formatted data from a basic parameter or entity field. 

long uprmgets (UHACT hParam, UCHAR** pData);

Parameters

hParam
A handle to the basic parameter or entity field
pData
(Output) The buffer containing the parameter or field data

Return Values

For more information, see the "Call-In API Return Codes" topic.

Use

Library: ucall

Description

Use the function uprmgets to get data from a parameter or entity field with a non-standard datatype that is not supported by one of the standard functions uuf2str, uuf2int, and so forth.

When you call uprmgets, data is allocated internally and a pointer to it is returned by reference. As usual with this type of function, your program is responsible for freeing that data when finished with it, using the function unifree. Not freeing the data can lead to memory leaks in your program.

The data in the allocated buffer is in internal Uniface format, which might not be easily readable for your program. In either case, you can put this data into another parameter by calling the uprmputs function.

Example: uprmgets and uprmputs

The following code uses uprmgets and uprmputs to pass output parameter data from one operation to the input parameter of another operation. The operations OP1 and OP2 are both located in the service TESTSVC, and are defined as follows:

operation op1
params
string msg : inout;
endparams
msg = "the quick brow fox jumps over the lazy dog"
end

operation op2
params
string msg : in;
endparams
putmess msg
end

Following is the 3GL code. Note that we could have used the detach/attach mechanism here, but then the program would not have had access to the Uniface-formatted data.

#include <h3gl.h>

void get_and_put2 ()
{
UHACT hEnv, hIn; /* Environment and instance handle */
UHACT hOp1, hOp2; /* Operation handles for OP1 amd OP2 */
long actStatus, procErr; /* Activation status and procerr */
UCHAR* pMsg; /* Pointer to string parameter data */
UHACT hPrm1, hPrm2; /* Parameter handles */

ueopen(&hEnv); /* Get environment handle */
uinstnew (hEnv, (UCHAR *)"TESTSVC", 0, 0,
UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, &procErr);/* Instantiate service TESTSVC*/

uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"OP1", 0, &hOp1); /* Instantiate operation OP1 */
uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"OP2", 0, &hOp2); /* Instantiate operation OP1 */

uopract (hOp1, &actStatus, &procErr);/* Activate operation OP1 */
uprmgeth(hOp1, 1, 0, &hPrm1); /* Get OP1 param handle */
uprmgets(hPrm1, &pMsg); /* Get Uniface-formatted data from OP1*/

uprmgeth(hOp2, 1, 0, &hPrm2); /* Get OP2 param handle */
uprmputs(hPrm2, pMsg); /* Put Uniface-formatted data into OP1*/
uopract (hOp2, &actStatus, &procErr); /* Activate operation OP2 */

uinstdel (hIn, 0); /* Destroy instance */
unifree (hOp1, pMsg); /* Free the string data */
ufreeh (&hOp1); /* Free OP1 operation handle */
ufreeh (&hOp2); /* Free OP2 operation handle */
ufreeh (&hPrm1); /* Free OP1 parameter handle */
ufreeh (&hPrm2); /* Free OP2 parameter handle */
ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free instance handle */
ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free environment handle */
}
Related Links
  • Call-In API Return Codes

    Codes that can possibly be returned by functions from the 3GL call-in API are consistent with the values returned by the ProcScript function $procerror, unless noted otherwise.

  • uprmputs

    Fill a basic parameter or entity field with Uniface-formatted data.

  • uuf2int

    Copy an integer value from a basic parameter or entity field to an int program variable.

  • uuf2str

    Get a pointer to the string data in a basic parameter or entity field.
